hs_description: Waste and scrap of electrical and electronic equipment
notes: HS 8549 introduced in HS 2022. Explicitly covers WEEE. Maps to 'all categories' — breakdown by category requires sub-code or national reporting. Supersedes 8548 in HS 2022+ data.

hs_description: Waste and scrap of primary cells primary batteries and electric accumulators; spent primary cells spent primary batteries and spent electric accumulators; electrical parts of machinery or apparatus
notes: HS 2017 and earlier. Covers waste of electrical/electronic equipment broadly. Used as the primary e-waste HS code before HS 8549 was introduced. In HS 2022 data, use 8549 instead.

hs_description: Analogue or hybrid automatic data-processing machines
notes: Mostly new goods. Waste fraction ~8% based on UN Monitor methodology (ratio of used/refurbished trade to total in HS chapter 84). Low volume relative to other IT codes.

hs_description: Portable automatic data-processing machines weighing not more than 10 kg (laptops/tablets)
notes: Laptops and portable computers. Significant secondhand/refurbished trade flow. Waste fraction ~12%. Common in exports from OECD to developing countries under 'secondhand goods' which may include end-of-life units.

hs_description: Other automatic data-processing machines comprising in the same housing at least a CPU and an input/output unit (desktops)
notes: Desktop computers. Waste fraction ~10%. Substantial secondhand exports to West Africa and Southeast Asia documented in BAN Tracker data.

hs_description: Other automatic data-processing machines (servers and computing units)
notes: Servers and computing units. Lower waste fraction than consumer devices (~6%). Enterprise upgrade cycles generate e-waste but are typically handled by IT asset disposition firms.

hs_description: Processing units other than those of subheading 8471.41 or 8471.49
notes: Processing units. Low waste fraction (~5%). Difficult to distinguish new from refurbished in trade data.

hs_description: Input or output units whether or not containing storage units in the same housing (monitors/keyboards)
notes: Includes CRT and LCD monitors. Historically significant e-waste category due to CRT toxicity. Waste fraction ~15% reflecting high volume of used monitor exports. Flag flows to non-OECD destinations.

hs_description: Storage units (hard drives/SSDs/optical drives)
notes: Storage devices. Waste fraction ~8%. Data security concerns around secondhand hard drives are documented in literature (Garfinkel et al. 2003 and subsequent studies).

hs_description: Other units of automatic data-processing machines
notes: Other computer peripherals. Waste fraction ~7%.

hs_description: Other data processing machines
notes: Catch-all for other ADP machines. Waste fraction ~7%.

hs_description: Duplicating machines (photocopiers)
notes: Photocopiers have high waste fraction (~20%) due to lease/replacement cycles. Large equipment by weight but categorized as small equipment in UN Monitor.

hs_description: Machines for sorting or folding mail or for inserting mail in envelopes or bands (mailing machines)
notes: Office mailing equipment. Waste fraction ~15%. Smaller market, lower absolute volume.

hs_description: Other office machines (cash registers/ATMs/check writing machines)
notes: Catch-all for office machines. Cash registers and ATMs have significant replacement cycles. Waste fraction ~12%.

hs_description: Parts and accessories of typewriters or word-processing machines
notes: Parts and accessories. Very low waste fraction (~3%) — mostly new spare parts trade.

hs_description: Electronic assemblies for calculating machines (HS 8470)
notes: Electronic assemblies. Low waste fraction.

hs_description: Other parts and accessories for calculating machines
notes: Parts for calculators. Low volume and low waste fraction.

hs_description: Parts and accessories for automatic data-processing machines and units (computer parts)
notes: Computer parts and circuit boards. Contains some e-waste (stripped motherboards/RAM) but mostly new components. Waste fraction ~5%. However: printed circuit boards (PCBs) are a significant informal recycling target — flag HIGH-volume flows to known informal processing destinations.

hs_description: Parts and accessories for other office machines (HS 8472)
notes: Office machine parts. Low waste fraction.

hs_description: Parts and accessories for multiple types of machines
notes: Mixed parts. Low waste fraction.

hs_description: Sorting screening separating or washing machines for earth stone ores or other mineral substances
notes: Industrial sorting machinery. Minimal e-waste fraction (~2%). Included because some e-waste shredding/sorting equipment is traded under this code but it is a small fraction.

hs_description: Crushing or grinding machines for earth stone ores or other mineral substances
notes: Industrial grinding machinery. Very low e-waste fraction.

hs_description: Concrete or mortar mixers
notes: Construction equipment. Negligible e-waste fraction. Included in pipeline for completeness but should produce near-zero volumes in practice.

hs_description: Machines for mixing mineral substances with bitumen
notes: Asphalt mixing machinery. Negligible e-waste fraction.

hs_description: Other mixing or kneading machines
notes: Industrial mixing equipment. Negligible e-waste fraction.

hs_description: Other machinery
notes: Other mineral processing machinery. Low e-waste fraction.

hs_description: Parts for the above
notes: Parts for mineral processing machinery. Negligible e-waste fraction.

hs_description: Machines for assembling electric or electronic lamps tubes or valves (lightbulb assembly)
notes: Lamp assembly machinery. Low waste fraction (~5%). Some e-waste embedded in obsolete lamp manufacturing equipment.

hs_description: Machines for making optical fibres and optical fibre preforms
notes: Optical fibre manufacturing equipment. Low e-waste fraction.

hs_description: Other machines for manufacturing glass or glassware
notes: Glass manufacturing equipment. Low e-waste fraction.

hs_description: Parts for the above
notes: Parts for lamp/glass machinery. Low e-waste fraction.

hs_description: Automatic beverage-vending machines incorporating heating or refrigerating appliances
notes: Refrigerated vending machines. Moderate waste fraction (~10%) due to refrigerant-containing equipment trade. Flag flows as they contain ozone-depleting substances in older units.

hs_description: Other automatic food/beverage vending machines
notes: Food vending machines. Waste fraction ~8%.

hs_description: Machines for selling postage stamps phone cards or similar articles
notes: Automated kiosks. Waste fraction ~10%. Includes ATM-type machines.

hs_description: Other vending machines
notes: Other vending machines. Waste fraction ~8%.

hs_description: Parts for vending machines
notes: Parts for vending machines. Low waste fraction.

hs_description: Injection-moulding machines (plastics)
notes: Industrial plastics machinery. Low e-waste fraction. Included because some cable insulation stripping equipment falls here.

hs_description: Extruders for working rubber or plastics
notes: Extrusion machinery. Low e-waste fraction. Cable recycling equipment may appear here.

hs_description: Blow-moulding machines
notes: Blow-moulding machinery. Negligible e-waste fraction.

hs_description: Vacuum moulding and other thermoforming machines
notes: Thermoforming machinery. Negligible e-waste fraction.

hs_description: Machinery for moulding or retreading pneumatic tyres (for rubber/plastics working)
notes: Tyre moulding machinery. Negligible e-waste fraction.

hs_description: Other machinery for moulding rubber or plastics
notes: Rubber/plastics moulding machinery. Negligible e-waste fraction.

hs_description: Other machinery for working rubber or plastics (includes cable granulators)
notes: Cable granulators and recycling machinery appear here. Waste fraction ~4% for the code overall but these specific sub-applications are relevant to e-waste processing. Track separately if granular HS6 data available.

hs_description: Parts for the above
notes: Parts for rubber/plastics machinery. Low e-waste fraction.

hs_description: Machinery for preparing or making up tobacco
notes: Tobacco machinery. Negligible e-waste fraction. Included for completeness.

hs_description: Parts for tobacco machinery
notes: Parts for tobacco machinery. Negligible e-waste fraction.

hs_description: Machinery for public works building or the like
notes: Construction machinery. Negligible e-waste fraction.

hs_description: Machinery for the extraction or preparation of animal or fixed vegetable fats or oils
notes: Food processing machinery. Negligible e-waste fraction.

hs_description: Presses for the manufacture of particle board or fibre building board of wood or other ligneous materials
notes: Press machinery. Negligible e-waste fraction.

hs_description: Rope or cable-making machines
notes: Cable-making machinery. Low e-waste fraction but relevant to e-waste processing infrastructure.

hs_description: Industrial robots not elsewhere specified or included
notes: Industrial robots. Low e-waste fraction (~3%). Replacement cycles generate e-waste but are typically managed by OEMs.

hs_description: Evaporative air coolers
notes: Evaporative coolers contain refrigerants in some configurations. Waste fraction ~8%.

hs_description: Passenger boarding bridges of a kind used in airports
notes: Airport infrastructure. Negligible e-waste fraction.

hs_description: Other machines and mechanical appliances
notes: Catch-all for other machinery. Low e-waste fraction.

hs_description: Mixing stirring kneading crushing grinding screening or separating machines (for other than minerals)
notes: Industrial process machinery. Low e-waste fraction.

hs_description: Mixing or kneading machines for mineral substances (not 8474)
notes: Mineral mixing machinery. Low e-waste fraction.

hs_description: Other machines and mechanical appliances having individual functions
notes: Broad catch-all. E-waste relevance is low but non-zero due to breadth of coverage.

hs_description: Parts for the above machinery
notes: Parts for miscellaneous machinery. Low e-waste fraction.
